This script calculates descriptive statistics (mean ± SEM, median, SD, n) for peak depolarisation amplitudes recorded during SLA experiments across different cell types (pericytes patched with low and high Cl⁻ intracellular solution, astrocytes, and [K⁺]o-sensitive potential).

# load data
df     <- read.csv("OHSC_SLA_Peak_Depolarisation_Change.csv")
groups <- split(df$max_amplitude_mV, df$sample_type)

# descriptive statistics
for (name in names(groups)) {
  x      <- na.omit(groups[[name]])
  n      <- length(x)
  sd_val <- sd(x)
  cat(sprintf("%s (n = %d)\n", name, n))
  cat(sprintf("Mean ± SEM = %.1f ± %.1f mV\n", mean(x), sd_val / sqrt(n)))
  cat(sprintf("Median     = %.1f mV\n", median(x)))
  cat(sprintf("SD         = %.2f mV\n", sd_val))
  cat(sprintf("\n"))
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
  hist(x, freq = FALSE, breaks = 10, col = "steelblue", border = "white",
       main = paste(name, "– Histogram"), xlab = "Peak depolarisation (mV)", ylab = "Density")
  qqnorm(x, main = paste(name, "– QQ-Plot"))
  qqline(x, col = "red")
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}
